steps.canonical_text: Tilt their head back gently and lift the chin to open the airway.
Look at the chest, listen, and feel for normal breathing for up to 10 seconds. Treat occasional gasps as not breathing.
If they are breathing normally, gently roll them onto their side into the recovery position. Do not move them if a back or neck injury is suspected.
Tilt the head slightly back and open the mouth so any fluids can drain. Stay with them and check breathing every minute.
If they stop breathing or you only see gasping, call emergency services now and start chest compressions.
